answer: The opening line of the poem 'Deor' (chapter 13 in Old English Elegies, work slug: unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e) names the smith Welund. The line reads: 'Welund him be wurman      wræces cunnade' — meaning 'Welund, the smith, experienced misery among serpents/snakes' (or 'endured exile among serpents'). This is the very first line of the poem and the one that introduces and names him.
